ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Laban aa no o manaara Jekɔb waa la, o chwao ka chemo dɛ ɡbaaro pɛl o te dɛ ba o ne dem. Asɛ Jekɔb bɔ o amaneɛ bwa.
translated: Laban gave his daughter to Jacob as a wife, and he worked for seven years, and afterward requested to marry her. However, Jacob had to fulfill his father-in-law's demand.

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Anee Lea ŋɔ, “Korowii tɛ mɛ n kembɔr, dɛkalkɛ n lawɛ n tolo tɛ n jaa asɛ o ne o chɔ.” Anomanta, o pa o sɔn Isaaka.
translated: And Leah said, "You have taken my maid, but now I will give my maid to my husband, and she will bear children for me." Then she gave her to her son Isaac.

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: O chɔɡ deŋɡarere dor harɛ waa chɔa kaa la bwa ne bwɛɛre aa lɔ o ta, nɛra baa lɔ bela bwa sekwaraa, ne konduula aa lɔ harɛ la ta bwa.
translated: When O Chog Degarere went to the river to fetch water, the pot fell into the river, the crocodile came out of the river, and the tortoise followed him to the river.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Belaa o ŋɔ, “N na Korowii siwi ne dɛ ta so mkpa ta.” Anomanta, o pa bela sɔn Pɛneɛl.
translated: Belaa and ŋɔ, "I am Korowii, the child he gave you to keep." Anomanta spoke to Pɛneɛl.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: O ŋɔ o bonɔɔ waa tɔ sie la be ŋɔ, “Dɛ n dɔɔ Esɔ cheme dɛ bɔɔre ŋɔ he dɔɔree umeo? Leperaa hɛɛ kaa lɔ? Ume kondɛɛraa he kpɔ dɛɛ kaa laa,
translated: He told them, "When you find him, tell him, 'Where is the child Esɔ that you are looking for? Did he give you water?' The leper said, 'Where?' The water in the village, he went where there was water."

ref: She had already hidden them in the cushion she used as a saddle and was sitting on it. Laban searched everywhere and did not find them.
text: Ajaah dɛ onee kpɔa do kon baa kpɔa dawɛ nyoɡma ta dɛɛ so o ta la lɔɡa dɛ so a ta. Dɛ Laban kaa nyiŋi o dea kɛ o ta an nae.

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Anee Rebɛka dɔɔ ne o nya chaŋe ŋɔ, “Gel haah la so ya be tama dɛ́ ɛ tɔpera fi mɔɔ dɛ́ kaa.”
translated: Then Rebecca quickly emptied her jar into the trough, "Let him drink, and I will also water his camels."

ref: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
text: Bii la bwa baa kpeɡ. Asɛ Esɔ kpɔ o te ɛ kpɔɡ bini nɛn dɛɛ kpaah kɛrɛdɛ. Dɛ Jekɔb te dɛ so dem.
translated: Bii went to drink water. And Esɔ saw him when he saw the child and the woman. Jacob was among them.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Awola webaŋkpela bɛl, Abraham soɡe o tɛnte dea doonyoa Mamre dajena la bɔr, asɛ Korowii berɛ ba o be ja.
translated: Abraham pitched his tent in the land of Canaan, and Korah's family also stayed there during that time.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: O kpale waa tɔ sie la sɔne Pesɔn Moo. O palɔ dɛ ɡoli kpɔ Havila harɛ ta bela suuɡaswɛm aa lole.
translated: He went to the land called Pesɔn Moo. He also went to Havila where there is a great gathering and teaching.

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Asɛ haah la chaŋo ŋɔ, “Dɛ́ ya won daa nɔ bɛlbwa nɔn dii.
translated: Asɛ said to them, "I will go there and take the children and the others."

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Kɛ mɛɛ ɡeloo n fɔo la do n ne he bie Aesek Saara aa dɛ ba o lola benanyoa saŋ nɔ bɔr la baah.”
translated: And Isaac said to Sarah, "Please say that you are my sister," so that it may go well with me because of you, and that my soul may live on account of you.

ref: The king went back to sleep and had another dream. This time seven full heads of grain were growing on a single stalk.
text: O berɔ chɔ dom, dɛ dee ja asɛ boyu anuanɛ sɛɡle daa beŋkpoŋ ta dɛ ta pure. A bwa ɡe mao kɛrɛdɛ.
translated: He went to the house and told them about the boy's dream at the big church in Beŋkpoŋ. I ate my good meal.

ref: Joseph's brothers killed a goat and dipped Joseph's fancy coat in its blood.
text: Anee ba kpo booh la bɛl, dɛ kpɔ Josef batakaare la mili o chal la,
translated: Anee went to the child's house, and Joseph called the father, he milked and drank there.

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
text: Pur bini nee la baa tonɛ o kpɔ bie la ka bil dajeɡa bɛl lɔɡa,
translated: When I went to the town, I bought a bag and came back home.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: O berɔ ŋɔ, <<Pɛ́ŋa ne nee bini koŋkwaa su nee la ta pirim ásɛ jumbii ne koŋɡaahla do nyundua dɛɛ ɡah.>>
translated: He said, "The farmer went to the farm to plant yams and cassava before the rain came."

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Awola webaŋkpela bɛl, Abraham soɡe o tɛnte dea doonyoa Mamre dajena la bɔr, asɛ Korowii berɛ ba o be ja.
translated: At the great river, Abraham journeyed to dwell in the land of Mamre in the Negev, and Korowii was there with him.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Dɛkalkɛ dɛ harɛ o ne Jekɔb aa soɡe o ta la aa kanɛ tɛɛ ba. Ba dɔ kondɛɛra asɛ a ya, anomanta, dɛ a chɛ kɛ bɛlbwa nyin bofale dɛ́ ka so o kwaa ne.
translated: Esau went to the field to prepare food for his father Isaac, while Jacob his son was cooking. When he returned from the field, having hunted, he wanted to eat the food, but his father said to him, "Who are you?"

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Kɛ he bɛ konlale di, dɛkalkɛ o sewɔ ta baŋa. Komɛlbwa mkpa lɔ o chal ta.
translated: And he spoke to it, Dekalkpe went to the town. Komɛlbwa tied the rope there.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Waa la kondikwaa la ne la, o yerɛ o mɛɛ, asɛ o sɛh dɛ pɛɛ ŋɔ, “Umee yɔ?”
translated: When he arrived at the village, he said to him, and he called him, and he sent him to that place, saying, "Umee, where are you going?"
